compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: DE-RISK E2 PASSED (warm-then-fan-out, the S3 load-bearing assumption): (a) COLD concurrent x3 sharing one cached system prompt -> 3 writes / 0 reads (the ~20x all-miss problem is REAL). (b) WARM one call to completion, then concurrent x3 -> 0 writes / 3 reads (all hit). Confirms: parallelize MUST warm first; warm-then-fan-out yields 1 write + (N-1) reads as designed. Threading + run_sync cache reads work correctly.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: DE-RISK D (offline, MAJOR-5) PASSED: with the CURRENT 2N container cost, shed_to_budget DROPS G3 at N=20 and G3+G4 at N=40 (child-coverage/consistency silently unreviewed on large epics — the fidelity regression). Halving the container cost (merge, S4) pushes the shed threshold past N=40, but it still eventually sheds => the remediation's stronger rule (container criteria NEVER shed; cap bounds only overlay/agent) is the robust fix, with merge as a major mitigation. Confirms both the bug and the fix direction.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: FORCE_CLAIM: plan-review gate bypassed by user approval — no plan-review attestation was verified. Reason: "Gate returns unsigned INDETERMINATE (8 plan-doc-quality nits, 0 BLOCK); implementation complete + independent subagent code review PASS + 275 tests green + parallel/cache behavior unit-tested. Binding quality gate = the code review.".

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
CONTAINER review (G4 — child consistency), parent c81c-cbcd-17a0-43d2 (plan-review fan-out optimization). Reviewed THIS child (S3 — parallelize container fan-out + warm-then-fan-out + budget-cap fix) WHOLE against the complete sibling roster (S1 0250, S2 c6e5, S4 98c6, S5 1762, S6 da34) and the parent. Checked all 7 cross-child interaction modes. NO G4 FINDINGS.

Affirmations (grounded in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/pass1.py:114-250 and sizing.py:60-71,190-242):
- IMPLICIT SHARED STATE: S3, S4, S5 all touch the sizing.py container-cost projection and the _run_container call structure (pass1.py). This is real shared state, but the parent's Sequencing note + DAG (S3->S4->S5) STRICTLY serialize it ("S4/S5's sizing.py container-cost projections are only correct AFTER S3's 'container never shed' + cost-model fix lands"). S3 is foundational/first; each later landing layers a correct projection on the prior (container_floor_usd: S3 = len(container)*n_children*COST_AGENT at sizing.py:232; S4 -> N; S5 -> <N). No contradiction.
- CONFLICTING ASSUMPTIONS: S3's warm-then-fan-out premise (warm WRITES the prefix, fan-out READS it) is the exact behavior S1 delivers and S1's de-risk E2 empirically confirmed (cold-concurrent x3 => 3 writes/0 reads; warm-first => 0 writes/3 reads). No sibling assumes G3/G4 can be shed; S3's "container NEVER shed" (sizing.py:198-206) is consistent with the parent fidelity AC and with S4/S5's eval-gating, which assume the container always runs. No conflict.
- DEPENDENCY GAP: S1 (0250) blocks ba7e (dep link present); ba7e blocks S4 (98c6) (dep link present). Byte-stability (an S1 AC) is the precondition S3's warm-then-fan-out relies on, correctly upstream. No gap.
- SCOPE OVERLAP: S3 fences out S1/S4/S5 and each fences S3 reciprocally. Shared files pass1.py (_run_container) and sizing.py are split as SERIAL layered edits under the strict S3->S4->S5 ordering (S3 builds the new pool + warm + never-shed/cost-model fix; S5 later adds bin-packing inside the same structure; S4/S5 take the sizing projection N->/<N), not concurrent contradictory ownership. Benign under ANTI-FP.
- ORDERING VIOLATION: none — S3 is correctly the HEAD of the S3->S4->S5 chain and the first to touch the shared projection; the Sequencing note pins this.
- CONSUMER IMPACT: S3's "container criteria are NEVER shed; the cap bounds only overlay/agent spend" + the cost-model correction is consumed (not contradicted) by S4/S5's projections and by the parent budget AC. The shed_to_budget code already reflects S3's guarantee (sizing.py:198-242: container returned unchanged, container_floor recorded for observability). Producer->consumer is DAG-ordered. Consistent.
- RESIDUAL REFERENCES: no sibling carries a stale contract against a design S3 removes. S3's budget-cap change removes the shed-G3/G4 path; no sibling references the centrality-inversion or assumes container shedding. Consistent.

Non-G4 note (intra-child editorial only, below the cross-child/high-confidence threshold — recorded, not a finding): the child's "What" bullet and original ACs still carry the SUPERSEDED framing — "await its first streamed token" (the runner is non-streaming) and "Fix the budget cap that scales with centrality (sizing.py base*(1+centrality))" implying centrality-inversion. The child's own Remediation explicitly reverses BOTH (warm = first real pairing to completion; do NOT invert centrality — keep base*(1.0+ctx.centrality) at sizing.py:71 and instead never-shed container + fix the cost model). These are stale body/AC labels superseded by the same ticket's Remediation, not a cross-child inconsistency; they read benignly under the ANTI-FP filter.

compiled_state.description:
# Parallelize the container fan-out with warm-then-fan-out + fix the centrality budget cap

## Why
The container loop is sequential (`2*N` calls ≈ 27 min wall-clock). Parallelizing fixes latency. But caching
(S1) is write-once/read-many — a NAIVE concurrent fan-out makes all calls MISS+WRITE (≈20x input cost, no read
benefit). So parallelization must WARM the cache first. Separately, the per-plan budget cap scales UP with child
count (centrality), disabling shedding for exactly the large epics that need it.

## What
- Move the container pairings out of the sequential `for crit: for child:` loop into the existing
  `ThreadPoolExecutor` (`pass1.py`).
- WARM-THEN-FAN-OUT: run ONE warming call first, await its first streamed token (the cache becomes readable once
  the first response begins), THEN fan out the remaining pairings concurrently so they READ the cached prefix
  rather than all re-writing it. (Optional: a pre-warm call at gate start.)
- Fix the budget cap that scales with centrality (`sizing.py` `base*(1+centrality)`) so shedding engages for
  large epics instead of being disabled by the very signal (more children) that should trigger it.

## Scope
- In: container parallelization; the warm-then-fan-out sequencing; the budget-cap fix.
- Out: the caching mechanism (S1, dependency); merge (S4); bin-pack (S5).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Container pairings run concurrently in the thread pool (not the sequential loop).
- [ ] Warm-then-fan-out: one call warms the cache before the fan-out; on anthropic the fanned-out calls show
      `cache_read_input_tokens > 0` (NOT all writes) — proven by a test/measurement.
- [ ] The per-plan budget cap no longer disables shedding as child count grows (a large epic sheds as intended).
- [ ] An 8-child epic review wall-clock drops to minutes; `make lint` + tests pass.

## Remediation (opus review — MAJOR 4/5)
- The runner is NON-streaming (`run_sync` blocks). Warm = run ONE pairing TO COMPLETION, then fan out the rest —
  not "await first streamed token". The warm pairing is the FIRST REAL pairing; keep its findings; fan out the
  remaining N-1; aggregate must equal the sequential baseline (no dup/drop).
- There is NO open ThreadPoolExecutor at `_run_container` (the Pass-1 pool closed at pass1.py:336). Create a NEW
  pool inside `_run_container`.
- Gate warming on `est_tokens(plan) >= 4096` (cache floor; reuse det_floor.est_tokens) — else fan out directly
  (no warm), since a sub-floor prefix never caches and warming would just add a serialized call.
- Warm-call failure: systemic (LLMUnavailableError) -> abort like the Pass-1 tier (pass1.py:327-331); non-systemic
  -> degrade to a direct fan-out (accept all-miss), never hang.
- Budget cap: do NOT invert centrality — shedding marks G3/G4 INDETERMINATE, so the naive "fix" would DROP
  child-coverage/consistency on the most central epics (a fidelity regression the epic AC forbids). Instead:
  container criteria are NEVER shed; the cap bounds only overlay/agent spend; correct the COST MODEL
  (sizing.py:206 `len(cont)*n_children*COST_AGENT`) rather than the centrality scaling.
- [ ] Warm = first real pairing run to completion; aggregate finding set equals the sequential baseline (no dup/drop).
- [ ] A NEW thread pool runs the remaining pairings concurrently; on anthropic the fanned-out calls show cache_read>0.
- [ ] Warming is skipped when est_tokens(plan) < 4096 (fan out directly); warm-call systemic failure aborts,
      non-systemic failure degrades to direct fan-out.
- [ ] Container criteria are NEVER shed by the budget cap; the cap bounds only overlay/agent spend (numeric target stated).

file_impact.reason: new pool inside _run_container + warm-then-fan-out + warm/floor/failure handling
container criteria never shed; correct the cost model (not centrality inversion)

compiled_state.title: Parallelize the container fan-out with warm-then-fan-out + fix the centrality budget cap
